Ownership & Access
Organizer-owned shell
Owned by the conference organizer/control-tower identity. Current owner UUID: conference-organizer.
Organizer/admin only. This shell may aggregate participant-derived insight, but it should not expose private participant state verbatim.
Organizer/admin only `dispatchAction` for KPI refresh, exports, sponsor follow-up, and operational tooling.
Available through admin-authenticated routes or Binding sessions resolved with the organizer identity.
Operational state, published program, consent-bound aggregate feeds, sponsor workflows, and derived KPI views are conference-owned artifacts.
Admin shells should orchestrate conference-owned state and derived insight. Raw participant entities remain outside this shell; organizer KPIs should consume explicit aggregate feeds or agreements instead.

Default query surface: Conference Participant Portal Dashboard.

Audience Discovery

Ownership & Access
Sponsor-owned shell
Owned by the sponsor or exhibitor identity that resolves `cell:///ConferenceSponsorShell`. Current owner UUID: 2EC6EF2A-E9B1-4A78-AEF1-8CF6779B798B.
Owner-only cell read. Organizer review should happen through handoff summaries and aggregates, not by reading sponsor follow-up shells directly.
Owner-only `dispatchAction` for qualification, unlock, reclaim, retention review, retention sweep, export, and inbox refresh inside sponsor-owned follow-up state.
Porthole and web preview can resolve this shell through `ConferenceSponsorPreviewShell`, while Binding can still resolve the same sponsor-owned truth directly with the sponsor identity.
LeadVault, ConsentReceipt, ExhibitorAccess, sponsor agreement refs, and sponsor chronicle are sponsor-owned artifacts once handoff is complete.
This shell is intentionally downstream from the organizer aggregate. It should only see what was explicitly handed off, consented, and materialized into sponsor-owned truth, and it must reclaim, review, and eventually purge sponsor-owned residue when retention policy requires it.
